requirements.goal: A browser-based EPUB reader workspace with library shelf, annotations, reading progress, and planning integration — shipped as a reusable React package.
requirements.audience: Portfolio visitors reading built-in EPUB editions, and workspace owners who import/upload personal EPUBs with persistent progress and highlights.

success-criteria.criterion: Render EPUB files from both remote URL and local ArrayBuffer sources using the react-reader library.
Support paginated (book-like spread) and scrolled-doc flow modes, toggled by a preferPagedReader setting.
Automatically switch between single-page and two-page spread layout based on viewport width (threshold ~1050px).
Apply a custom sepia-toned reader theme with serif typography to EPUB content frames.
Display a Table of Contents panel that slides in from the left with animated transitions (framer-motion), showing nested TOC items.
Show current section label, page number, and total pages in a bottom navigation bar.
Provide prev/next page navigation buttons and direct page-number jump input.
Calculate reading progress as a percentage from EPUB location data (CFI-based).
Restore reading position on return — persist last location per book using a storage key.
Support deep-linking to a specific EPUB location via URL search params (book, record, at, cfi).
Display a library shelf view with book cards in a responsive grid (2-col on sm, 3-col on xl).
Each shelf card shows cover image (or generated empty cover with initials), title, author, genres, description, and reading status badge.
Reading status badges resolve to New, N% progress, Done, or Coming Soon based on stored progress.
Partition shelf into built-in books (with EPUBs), queued books (no EPUB yet), and uploaded library books.
Filter the shelf by genre chips (All + distinct genres extracted from book entries).
Filter the shelf by free-text search across title, slug, author, description, and genres.
Drag-and-drop EPUB files onto the library view to import them.
Import EPUB via file picker button, validating .epub extension.
Format uploaded file name into a display title (strip extension, replace separators with spaces).
Render a collapsible sidebar rail on desktop (expanded: 15.5rem, collapsed: 4.25rem) with animated width transition.
Show a slide-over mobile drawer on viewports below md, with backdrop overlay and body scroll lock.
Sidebar shows Library link, Now Reading indicator when a book is open, and optional host-provided nav links with contextual icons.
Sidebar collapse/expand state persists in localStorage via a zustand store.
Allow text selection in the EPUB to create highlights with amber overlay styling.
Show a floating selection toolbar with Add Highlight and Dismiss actions when text is selected.
Display a Notes and Highlights side panel (slides from right) listing all annotations with quoted text and editable note fields.
Support Go To navigation (jump to annotation CFI) and Remove for each annotation.
Export annotations as a JSON file following the portfolio-epub-annotations schema.
Import annotations from a JSON file and re-apply highlights to the rendition.
Download an annotated EPUB with annotations embedded in META-INF/portfolio-annotations.json.
Persist annotations locally in IndexedDB keyed by storageKey + contentHash (SHA-256 of EPUB bytes).
Merge local, embedded, and remote annotations using last-write-wins by updatedAt timestamp.
Track reading progress and location in a zustand store persisted to localStorage as a single JSON blob (portfolio-reader-epub-reading).
Migrate legacy per-book localStorage keys (epub-location-*, epub-progress-*) into the consolidated store on first access.
Cross-tab synchronization — rehydrate the reading store when the storage event fires for the store key.
Support an optional ReaderPersistenceAdapter for remote save/load of location, progress, and annotations.
Debounce remote persistence writes by 500ms to avoid excessive API calls.
Owner workspace mode with access state controlling canPersist, canEdit, canUpload permissions.
Workspace settings panel (default view, prefer paged reader, show progress badges) persisted via host callback.
Continue-reading mode: when defaultWorkspaceView is continue-reading, auto-select the first book with saved progress on load.
Explicit upload panel for saving an imported EPUB to the backend library with title, author, description, visibility fields.
Upload requires explicit action — importing does not auto-upload; a separate Upload to Library flow is gated by canUpload.
Planning cockpit modal rendered as a full-screen overlay with backdrop blur, managed by a dedicated zustand store.
Modal accepts a host-provided renderPlanningCockpit render function and a ReaderPlanningCockpitPayload.
Collapsible planning strip bar between header and content area, showing Planning and Planning Cockpit buttons.
Expandable quick-links row within the planning strip using animated height transition.
Extract planning packs from EPUB files by reading a planning-pack manifest or falling back to plan-*.xhtml files.
Parse XHTML content into plain text for planning pack files, stripping HTML tags and decoding entities.
Lazy-load the EpubViewer component using React.lazy with a Suspense fallback showing a loading spinner.
Provide a Download EPUB button for built-in books, constructing the download URL from slug or remoteEpubUrl.
All workspace chrome uses semantic Tailwind/shadcn tokens (bg-background, border-border, text-foreground) — no separate light/dark palettes.

non-goals.item: The reader does NOT include a full CMS or book authoring interface — it is read-only with import.
The reader does NOT handle EPUB building/compilation — that is the repub-builder CLI concern.
The reader does NOT implement authentication — it accepts access state from the host.
The reader does NOT render non-EPUB formats (PDF, MOBI, etc.).

core-systems.system: EPUB rendering engine wrapping react-reader (epubjs). Handles book loading (URL fetch or ArrayBuffer), rendition configuration (theme, flow, spread), location tracking, TOC extraction, page calculation, and text selection events. Provides prev/next navigation and page jump.

Top-level workspace layout orchestrating the sidebar, header toolbar, planning strip, content area, and modal root. Manages EPUB import (file picker + drag-drop), workspace state resolution, and child component composition.

Library view with searchable, filterable book card grid. Partitions books by source kind and EPUB availability. Each card shows metadata, cover, status badge, and action controls. Cards link to the reader view for that book.

Collapsible navigation rail with desktop aside and mobile slide-over drawer. Shows brand header, Library link, Now Reading section, and host-injected navigation links. Collapse state persisted in a UI store.

Highlight and note system. Creates highlights from text selections on the EPUB rendition. Persists to IndexedDB keyed by book+hash. Supports JSON import/export and embedding annotations inside EPUB ZIP archives. Merges annotations from multiple sources (local, embedded, remote) using timestamp-based conflict resolution.

Zustand store (persist + immer middleware) tracking per-book reading progress (0-1 float) and last CFI location. Single localStorage JSON blob with legacy key migration. Cross-tab sync via storage event listener.
Zustand store persisting sidebar expanded state and shelf genre filter. Includes legacy key migration from older localStorage formats.
Adapter interface for optional remote persistence. Accepts loadState/saveState with storageKey + contentHash. Merges remote state with local on hydration. Debounced writes.
Zustand store for planning cockpit modal (open/close + payload). ReaderModalRoot renders the overlay with host-provided content function.
Planning strip with quick-links and cockpit button. Planning pack extraction from EPUB archives. Host-configurable per-book planning payload.

Lightweight UI primitives (Button, Card, Badge, Input, Select, Textarea, Checkbox) with variant/size props and a minimal cn() class merge utility. Not shadcn/ui directly — custom implementations matching shadcn conventions.
Single object mapping semantic slot names to Tailwind class strings. All workspace components reference this map for consistent theming.
Utility function constructing reader URLs from a base path and optional search params (book, record, at, cfi).

stack.item: React 19 — component UI and hooks for state/effects
TypeScript — strict typing for all modules, exported type definitions for host integration
react-reader v2 (epubjs wrapper) — EPUB rendering, TOC, location management, rendition API
zustand v5 with persist + immer middleware — client-side state management with localStorage persistence
framer-motion — animated transitions for TOC panel, notes panel, and planning strip
lucide-react — icon library for all UI icons
jszip — reading/writing ZIP archives for annotation embedding and planning pack extraction
Tailwind CSS — utility-first styling with shadcn semantic token conventions
tsup — build tooling for the package (ESM output)
